questionText: What do MIN() and MAX() do?

codeSnippet: SELECT * FROM employees WHERE salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employees)

SELECT * FROM departments d WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM employees e WHERE e.department_id = d.id)

questionText: What do ANY and ALL do?
codeSnippet: WHERE salary > ANY (SELECT salary FROM managers)
SELECT name FROM employees UNION SELECT name FROM managers

SELECT name FROM employees INTERSECT SELECT name FROM managers
SELECT name FROM employees EXCEPT SELECT name FROM managers
options: Rows in first not in second

codeSnippet: SELECT name, salary, AVG(salary) OVER (PARTITION BY department) FROM employees
ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY salary DESC) as rank
NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY salary) as quartile

questionText: What do LAG and LEAD do?

questionText: What do FIRST_VALUE and LAST_VALUE do?
codeSnippet: FIRST_VALUE(salary) OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY hire_date)
WITH cte AS (SELECT * FROM employees) SELECT * FROM cte
WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT n+1 FROM cte WHERE n < 10)
CREATE VIEW employee_names AS SELECT name FROM employees
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW emp_summary AS SELECT department, COUNT(*) FROM employees
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_email ON employees(email);
CREATE INDEX idx_name_dept ON employees(name, department);
BEGIN TRANSACTION; UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance - 100; COMMIT;
EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM employees WHERE salary > 50000;
CREATE TABLE employees (id INT) PARTITION BY RANGE (year)
CREATE PROCEDURE GetEmployees() BEGIN SELECT * FROM employees; END
CREATE FUNCTION calculate_bonus(salary DECIMAL) RETURNS DECIMAL BEGIN RETURN salary * 0.1; END
CREATE TRIGGER update_timestamp BEFORE UPDATE ON employees FOR EACH ROW SET NEW.updated_at = NOW();
DECLARE cur CURSOR FOR SELECT name FROM employees; OPEN cur; FETCH cur INTO name;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT department, salary FROM employees) PIVOT (AVG(salary) FOR department IN ('IT', 'HR'))
SELECT * FROM (SELECT 'IT' as dept, it_salary FROM pivoted) UNPIVOT (salary FOR department IN (it_salary))
